agent_supports: no
daemon_capability: n/a; driver_capability: n/a

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: no
daemon_capability: n/a; driver_capability: n/a

## Reasoning

The Architect/Editor model-pair pattern is a feature of Aider (`--architect` mode) and a handful of other adapters that explicitly run two models per turn — one strong reasoning model produces a plan, a cheaper editor model emits the edits. Codex's planning + executor split and Continue's chat+edit are the other listed examples in the spec.

Claude Code has no such feature. The `claude` CLI uses exactly one model per turn (the one selected via `--model`, `/model`, or the default). There is no `--architect` flag, no two-model-per-turn flow, and no event in the JSONL transcript that would carry a second model contribution within one turn. The Task / Agent subagent tool spawns a separate child session with its own transcript and its own model field — that is a different scenario (foreground-subagent), not an in-turn architect/editor pair, because (a) the parent's `ready → working → ready` arc wraps the entire subagent run as one user-visible turn, and (b) the child session is observable as a distinct row.

Because agent_supports = no, irrlicht_observes is `n/a` per the matrix convention. There is nothing to observe; the parser code has no architect/editor code path; the spec bullets (two model contributions within one turn, session ModelName reflects architect) cannot be exercised because no input transcript will ever contain them.

This is the clearest 'no' of the seven cells in this batch and aligns with the prompt's quick-reference guidance.

## What irrlicht does NOT see (and why that's OK)

Everything. The feature does not exist in the agent under test, so the absence of observation is correct — not a gap. A future Claude Code release that introduced something like a planner/editor split would change this verdict, but as of the assessed date the answer is unambiguously no.
caveats: Do not confuse Claude Code's Task/Agent subagent tool with architect/editor: subagents are separate sessions with their own transcripts, while architect/editor is two model contributions inside one turn of one session. The spec is explicit that architect/editor lives at L443-450.
The matrix row for this cell should be marked with agent_supports=no and the recipe step skipped entirely (no recording, no expected.jsonl, no transcript.jsonl).

sources.note: Parser tracks one Model per requestId/turn via PerTurnContribution.Model — there is no in-turn second-model contribution path. extractClaudeCodeModel returns a single string.
Feature: Architect/Editor model pair — explicitly lists Aider --architect, Codex planning+executor, Continue chat+edit as the supporting agents. Claude Code is not in that list.
Claude Code CLI documentation lists --model as the sole model selector; no architect-mode flag exists in the public CLI surface as of the assessed date.
